summary: Frontend-only regression test for the bug fix that adds a 'Meet Dr. Ali' link to the site navbar (desktop + mobile). All requested scenarios verified via Playwright against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.
critical_code_review_comments: Header.jsx: mobile menu link click uses onClick={() => setOpen(false)} which fires before the native anchor jump; smooth-scroll behavior still works because href='#doctor' + CSS scroll-behavior:smooth. No issue observed.
NAV array uses href.slice(1) to derive data-testid, so a href of '#hours & location' would produce a spaced testid. Current values are safe ('#location' etc.) - just a maintenance note.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Static React landing page (Thompson Square Medical Centre / Dr. Aliya Ali). Only harmless GET /api/ ping on mount. Nav testids: nav-about, nav-services, nav-doctor, nav-reviews, nav-location (and mobile-nav-* mirrors). Header data-testid=site-header, mobile toggle=mobile-menu-toggle, header-call-btn (tel: link). Doctor section id='doctor'. Welcome modal appears on load with close via [aria-label='Close'] / [data-testid='welcome-modal-close'].

verification_details.welcome_modal: opens on load; closes via close button (verified)
